title: Stakeholders, Ethics, Accessibility, and Harm Analysis · Stakeholder Product Defense
summary: An affected-community, ethics, accessibility, and academic review panel. The panel needs a traceable mastery contract, significant durable problem case, stakeholder power map, benefit-burden evidence, harm controls, and explicit review routes. Planned artifact: Deliver Capstone Proposal and Significance Defense with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Map every claimed competency to prerequisite evidence, new transfer task, artifact, verification, counterexample, and defense question.
Triangulate direct research, domain artifacts, credible data, existing services, and counterevidence to establish problem significance.
Connect intervention to behavior and outcome through an explicit causal hypothesis with confounders and falsification evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Project Competency Claim Map
steps.instruction:
project case — Primary-user convenience shifts surveillance, exclusion, labor, safety, environmental, and appeal costs onto people with less product power. The project variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Project Competency Claim Map.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map every claimed competency to prerequisite evidence, new transfer task, artifact, verification, counterexample, and defense question. For Commit to the mission model · Project Competency Claim Map, keep Competency Claim Map connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Primary-user convenience shifts surveillance, exclusion, labor, safety, environmental, and appeal costs onto people with less product power. The project variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Competency Claim Map condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: GOV.UK Service Manual discovery and alpha guidance — Controls problem-first discovery, affected-user and constraint research, measures of success, riskiest-assumption prototypes, and evidence-based stop decisions before production building.

options.text: State the Competency Claim Map preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
One complex artifact automatically demonstrates every skill used to create it. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Competency Claim Map.

For commit to the mission model, apply Competency Claim Map without its stated preconditions, omit the stakeholder benefit-burden and harm register trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Competency Claim Map for stakeholder benefit-burden and harm register.
